2606.20852.title: Translating Inference-Time Control to Radiology Vision-Language Models: Activation Steering for Pneumonia Classification on Chest X-rays
2606.20852.paper_summary: Inference-time engineering can alter model behavior without fine-tuning. However, its utility for improving diagnostic performance in medical vision-language models (VLMs) remains unclear. We aim to evaluate whether Contrastive Activation Addition (CAA) can improve pneumonia classification in chest radiograph VLMs without updating model weights. Three frozen chest radiograph VLMs (MedGemma-4B-IT, NV-Reason-CXR-3B, and CheXOne-3B) were evaluated on the public Kermany pneumonia test set. Classification was based on the logits of the tokens Yes and No under a binary prompt. Steering vectors included a 30-pair answer-bias control, a 30-pair pneumonia text contrast, and an image-conditioned contrast derived from 30 pneumonia and 30 normal development images. A deterministic 200-image development set was used for layer and scale selection (100 images) and threshold calibration (100 images). Performance was assessed using ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, F1 score, threshold analyses, reverse-vector controls, random-vector controls, and conditional bootstrap confidence intervals. Fixed-threshold F1 improvements were frequently observed but did not consistently indicate improved diagnostic performance. For MedGemma-4B-IT. NV-Reason-CXR-3B showed the strongest benefit: calibrated F1 improved from 0.7692 in the zero-shot setting to 0.8619 with pneumonia-text steering and to 0.8727 with image-conditioned steering. For CheXOne-3B, pneumonia-text steering increased calibrated F1 from 0.8528 to 0.8666, although the confidence interval crossed zero. On this public pneumonia benchmark, CAA substantially altered prediction score distributions and operating characteristics without fine-tuning. Meaningful performance gains were observed in one of three evaluated VLMs, suggesting that activation steering may serve as a lightweight approach for adapting medical VLM behavior.
2606.20852.paper_summary_zh: 推理時間工程可以在不進行微調的情況下改變模型行為。然而，它在改善醫療視覺-語言模型（VLMs）診斷性能方面的效用仍不明確。我們旨在評估對比激活添加（CAA）是否能在不更新模型權重的情況下改善胸部X光VLMs中的肺炎分類。三個凍結的胸部X光VLMs（MedGemma-4B-IT、NV-Reason-CXR-3B和CheXOne-3B）在公開的Kermany肺炎測試集上進行了評估。分類基於二元提示下的令牌“是”和“否”的logits。引導向量包括30對答案偏差控制、30對肺炎文本對比以及從30張肺炎和30張正常發展圖像中衍生的圖像條件對比。一個確定性的200張圖像開發集被用於層和比例選擇（100張圖像）及閾值校準（100張圖像）。性能評估使用ROC-AUC、PR-AUC、F1分數、閾值分析、反向向量控制、隨機向量控制和條件自助信賴區間。固定閾值的F1改進經常被觀察到，但並未一致地指示出改善的診斷性能。對於MedGemma-4B-IT，NV-Reason-CXR-3B顯示出最強的好處：經校準的F1從零樣本設置的0.7692提高到使用肺炎文本引導的0.8619，並在圖像條件引導下提高到0.8727。對於CheXOne-3B，肺炎文本引導將經校準的F1從0.8528提高到0.8666，儘管信賴區間跨越了零。在這個公開的肺炎基準上，CAA顯著改變了預測分數分佈和運行特性，而無需微調。在三個評估的VLMs中，觀察到有意義的性能提升，這表明激活引導可能作為一種輕量級的方法來調整醫療VLM的行為。
2606.20852.author: Eduardo Moreno Judice de Mattos Farina et.al.
2606.20852.authors: Eduardo Moreno Judice de Mattos Farina, Mateus A. Esmeraldo, Felipe Akio Matsuoka, Paulo Eduardo de Aguiar Kuriki, Felipe Campos Kitamura
